title: 谋杀启事
summary:
During the investigation, Miss Blacklock recounts an encounter with a man named Sherris, who appeared ten days prior claiming he needed money to return to Switzerland because his mother was ill. Miss Blacklock, a cautious former secretary to a financier, refused his request because she suspected he was dishonest. She believes Sherris visited the house to scout the layout, noting his suspicious interest in the dining room and the front door latch. Miss Blacklock also mentions she had recently used a side door to shut in some ducks, leaving the status of the door's locks uncertain.

The discussion shifts to the events of the previous night. Miss Blacklock explains that at 6:30, while the lamps were lit, the lights suddenly went out. A masked man with a revolver entered through a door and demanded that everyone raise their hands. While Miss Bunner and others complied, Miss Blacklock did not. A flashlight beam blinded Miss Blacklock, followed by a gunshot that hit the wall behind her. She then felt a searing pain in her ear and heard a second shot. In the ensuing chaos and darkness, the intruder stumbled and a third shot was fired.

Miss Bunner insists the intruder was targeting Miss Blacklock as revenge for the refused money, suggesting the man shot himself after missing her. Miss Blacklock disputes the suicide theory and mentions she had previously suspected Patrick Simmons might be involved in a joke related to a murder advertisement. Despite the tension, Miss Blacklock attempts to calm a trembling Miss Bunner, who becomes distracted by household chores and a missing pillowcase.
